My changes to the `switch` statement there, specifically around the cast instructions, are causing the compiler to choke.

Initially, I tried brute-forcing a fix using `sed`. I thought I could surgically insert the necessary curly braces to encapsulate the new logic and its variable declarations within the `case` blocks. But every attempt – and there were many, each with a variation on line numbers, pattern matching, and block replacements – failed miserably. I'm basically jumping over variable declarations in the previous `case` statements, like the one for `Instruction::ExtractElement`.

My previous attempts with `sed` clearly failed because I needed to make changes in several different places to handle the various cast instructions and create new scopes. It just wasn't suited to the task, and the error logs confirmed it - the compiler pointed to the exact lines that were causing the problem.

The solution is straightforward, conceptually: add curly braces around the cast instruction blocks. But, I've learned the hard way, that doing so manually is error prone and using `sed` to do it is even worse. So, I will create a proper `patch` file and apply it. `patch` is the right tool. It is designed for this sort of precise and robust change. I'll craft a `diff` that will replace the existing, single-line `CreateCast` statements with the new, bracketed blocks, along with my fix. Then it's a simple `patch -p1 < myfix.patch` to integrate the changes cleanly and correctly. Time to generate that patch file.
